summary: This cluster of research focuses on the biotechnological potential of the green microalga *Coelastrella rubescens* (Scenedesmaceae, Chlorophyta) for the co-production of high-value keto-carotenoids and biofuels. Studies demonstrated that stress-induced secondary carotenogenesis in this species yields significant amounts of astaxanthin and canthaxanthin, while its lipid fatty acid profile is suitable for biodiesel synthesis. The investigations utilized CO₂ and sodium acetate under low-pH conditions to trigger these metabolic responses, highlighting a dual application in nutraceuticals and renewable energy. The geographic and ecological scope is not explicitly defined, but the work positions *C. rubescens* as a promising candidate for industrial biotechnology.
